settings.title: Settings
settings.viewerPrefsDesc: Defaults for the X-ray viewer and inference engine.
settings.appearanceDesc: Theme and language for this device.
settings.measurementHelp: Bone-loss measurements (mm/%) need a calibrated pixel scale and landmark detection that 2D X-rays don't carry. 3D (CBCT/IOS) measurements are always shown in millimeters.
settings.phiDefaultLabel: Show patient identifying info in dashboard and viewer
settings.confHelp: Lower = more sensitive (more findings, more false positives).
settings.toolsDesc: Enable experimental features for your account.
settings.enable3dHelp: Adds CBCT and intraoral-scan (IOS) viewing and segmentation. Off by default — the app stays X-ray and photo only until you turn this on.
settings.enablePhotoHelp: Show the intraoral camera (Photo) modality across upload and the patient record. Off by default.
settings.enablePanoramicHelp: Offer the panoramic modality on upload and enable the FMX (full-mouth series) view. Off by default — with it off, the FMX view is disabled.

localData.title: Local data
localData.desc: Your patients and studies are stored only on this device. Back them up to keep a copy on our servers (or in a file) so you can restore them on another device or after clearing your browser.
localData.noBackup: No backup found to restore from.
localData.localNewer: The data on this device is newer than the backup, so it was kept (nothing was overwritten).
localData.exportTooLarge: This dataset is too large for a .zip export (4 GB limit) — use "Back up to server" instead.
localData.failed: Something went wrong. Please try again.
localData.mergeIntro: The backup was compared with the data on this device:
localData.mergeAdds: New from the backup: {patients} patients, {studies} studies, {state} reports/markups
localData.mergeUpdates: Also update {n} items where the backup is newer
localData.mergeSuppressed: {n} items you deleted on this device will not be re-added
localData.mergeDuplicates: Possible duplicate patients — review them after merging:
localData.mergeNoDelete: Merge adds the backup's items to this device. Nothing on this device is deleted.
localData.replaceWarning: Replace overwrites ALL patient data on this device with the backup's contents.
localData.replaceBlockedLocalNewer: Replace is disabled: the data on this device is newer than the backup.
localData.mergeDone: Merge complete — {n} items added or updated.
localData.alreadyUpToDate: Already up to date — nothing to merge.
localData.storageFull: Not enough storage on this device for this merge.
localData.damagedIntro: This backup file is damaged: {lost} of {total} studies cannot be recovered. Everything else can still be merged.
localData.damagedReplaceBlocked: Replace is unavailable for a damaged file — only the recoverable items can be merged.

consent.subtitle: We've updated our terms. Please review and accept to continue.
consent.body: BeCertain Dx Viewer is intended as a clinical decision support tool. AI findings are advisory, not diagnostic. All clinical decisions remain the responsibility of the licensed practitioner.
consent.confirmIntro: By using this product, you confirm:
consent.confirm1: You are a licensed dental practitioner or working under direct supervision.
consent.confirm2: You will not rely on AI output as the sole basis for diagnosis or treatment.
consent.confirm3: You will protect patient PHI as required by HIPAA and applicable regional laws.
consent.confirm4: You consent to anonymized image analysis on our AI infrastructure.
consent.verifyErrorDesc: We couldn't confirm your consent status. Please check your connection and try again.

account.profileDesc: Your contact info, used for receipts and account recovery.
account.errNameTooLong: Name must be 100 characters or fewer

signup.title: Create your account

signup.errAcceptTerms: You must accept the terms of service
signup.errNameTooLong: Name must be 100 characters or fewer

forgot.title: Forgot password?
forgot.subtitle: We'll email you a reset link.
forgot.sent: If an account exists for {email}, a reset link has been sent.

legal.terms1Body: BeCertain Dx Viewer is a clinical decision-support tool. AI output is advisory, not diagnostic. All clinical decisions remain the responsibility of the licensed practitioner.
legal.terms2Body: You must be a licensed dental professional or working under the supervision of one.
legal.terms3Body: Uploaded radiographs and intraoral scans are processed by our AI infrastructure. We do not sell PHI. Images may be retained for service improvement after de-identification, unless you opt out.
legal.terms4Body: Subscriptions renew automatically until canceled. Refunds within 14 days. All amounts in test-mode currency during evaluation.
legal.terms5Body: No reverse engineering of AI models. No use for unauthorized patient data. No re-distribution of outputs to third parties.
legal.terms6Body: BeCertain disclaims liability for clinical outcomes. Maximum liability is limited to fees paid in the prior 12 months.
legal.privacy1Body: We collect account information (email, name, optional phone and address), billing information (processed by Stripe — we never see card numbers), and clinical content you upload (radiographs, CBCT volumes, intraoral scans, AI inference output, and any annotations you make). We log standard request metadata (IP, user-agent, timestamps) for security and rate-limiting.
legal.privacy2Body: Clinical content is processed by our AI inference service to produce the segmentation, detection, and report output you see in the viewer. Account data is used to authenticate you and to communicate about your subscription. Billing data is processed by Stripe under their own terms.
legal.privacy3Body: We do not sell PHI. We do not share clinical content with third parties for marketing or advertising. We share data with subprocessors strictly to operate the service (Stripe for payments, our cloud hosting provider for storage and compute, our AI inference provider). Each subprocessor is bound by data-processing agreements consistent with applicable law.
legal.privacy4Body: With your consent, de-identified images may be retained to improve model accuracy. You can opt out at any time in Settings; opting out applies to future uploads.
legal.privacy5Body: Active accounts: clinical content is retained while your subscription is active. Closed accounts: content is purged within 90 days of cancellation, unless retention is required for legal or audit reasons. Audit logs are retained for up to 12 months.
legal.privacy6BodyPre: You may request a copy of your data, correction of inaccurate data, or deletion of your account at any time by writing to
legal.privacy6BodyPost: . Depending on your jurisdiction (GDPR, CCPA, UK DPA), additional rights apply, including the right to object to processing and to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority.
legal.privacy7Body: Data is encrypted in transit (TLS) and at rest. Per-user file access is enforced by signed file tokens with short expiry. We follow the principle of least privilege for staff access and review controls regularly.
legal.privacy8Body: The service is for licensed dental practitioners; we do not knowingly create accounts for children. Patient images that you upload may include minors as part of legitimate clinical care — they are treated identically to other clinical content and not used for advertising.
legal.privacy9Body: Your data may be processed in countries other than the one where you are located. Where required, we rely on standard contractual clauses or equivalent safeguards.
legal.privacy10Body: We may update this policy as the product evolves. Material changes will be announced via the email on file and in-product before they take effect.
legal.privacy11BodyPre: Questions about this policy or your data:

ctl.screenCapture: Press Ctrl/Cmd+V with nothing to paste, then pick a screen or window to capture
ctl.classToggle: Show or hide one finding class
ctl.fmxSlot: Open the X-ray that fills a full-mouth-series position
ctl.dateFilter: Filter the studies by capture date
ctl.wheelScrub: Scroll to change slice (Ctrl/Cmd+scroll to zoom)
ctl.arrowSlice: ↑ / ↓ : scrub the slice in the pane under the cursor
ctl.orbit: Drag to orbit · scroll to zoom · right-drag to pan
ctl.orientGizmo: Snap the camera to an anatomical view
ctl.selectTooth: Click a tooth to select and highlight it

help.title: Dx Viewer user manual
help.subtitle: A guided tour of every part of Dx Viewer, organised by what you want to do. Each section has a screenshot and explains what the controls do and what you get back. The interface follows the language and theme you pick in Settings.
help.downloadPdf: Download this manual as a PDF
help.support: Need a hand? Email support@becertain.ai. Dx Viewer is AI-assisted and for clinician review — it is not a substitute for professional diagnosis.

signin.title: Signing in
signin.body: Enter your email and password and select Sign in. Prefer a one-time code? Choose “Sign in with email code”, enter the 6-digit code we email you, then Verify and sign in — switch back any time with “Use password instead”. New here? “Create account” opens sign-up and “Forgot password?” emails a reset link. The first time you sign in you're asked to review and accept the terms: I agree continues, Decline & sign out logs you out.

signup.title: Creating an account
signup.body: Enter your full name, work email and a password (at least 8 characters, typed twice to confirm), tick the box to accept the Terms of Service and Privacy Policy, then select Create account. You're taken straight to billing to choose a plan.

workspace.title: Finding your way around
workspace.body: The left rail is your home base: the logo returns to the dashboard, the arrow collapses the rail to icons, Help opens this manual, the sun/moon switches light and dark, and your initials open a menu with Account, Billing, Settings and Log out. The top bar has a search box (filter patients by name or date of birth), a clock that lists recently viewed studies (with Clear), and the PHI button that hides or shows patient-identifying details — useful when sharing your screen.

studies.title: Your studies dashboard
studies.body: Every patient is a card showing initials, name, date of birth, study count and last capture date — select a card to open that patient. New Study (top right) starts an upload. Type in the search box to filter; if nothing matches you can Clear search, and if the list fails to load a Retry button appears.

newStudy.title: Starting a new study
newStudy.body: Pick the modality — X-Ray, Panoramic, CBCT (3D), IOS Scan or Photo. Type the patient's name; matching existing patients appear underneath, and picking one files the study under that record (no duplicate). Enter a date of birth for a new patient. For a phone photo of an X-ray monitor, tick the auto-crop box. Drop your file on the dashed area or Browse to choose it (Remove clears it), then Run analysis. Cancel returns to the dashboard.

quickAnalyze.title: Quick analysis — drag, paste or capture
quickAnalyze.body: You don't have to open the upload form. Drag any file into the window and analysis starts the moment you drop it. Or copy an image or 3D file and paste it with Ctrl/Cmd+V. Press Ctrl/Cmd+V with nothing to paste and Dx Viewer asks which screen or window to capture, then analyses that frame as an X-ray. A quick scan is filed under a temporary patient; the banner that appears lets you Name patient, Add to existing, or Keep as-is.

xrayTools.title: Reading a 2D X-ray
xrayTools.body: The toolbar adjusts the image: zoom in/out, fit to screen, 100% actual size, a magnifier loupe, rotate, flip, brightness/contrast/sharpness/saturation (sliders open in a panel, with Reset), invert, a ruler to measure, and toggles for tooth numbers and anatomy. Drag to pan, scroll to zoom, and switch between FDI and Universal numbering. Across the top: Back, the FMX switch, the X-rays and Photos tabs and the PHI toggle; the side arrows — or the ← and → keys — move to the previous and next X-ray.

findings.title: The AI findings panel
findings.body: The right panel groups the AI's findings under Diagnostic Results. The master Hide All / Show All switch toggles the whole overlay, and each group has its own Hide / Show; expand a group to see the individual findings and hover one to highlight it on the image. Switch between By disease and By tooth. Conditions with no findings are hidden, and the tool rail's add-detection tools let you add or hide a detection yourself.

fmxNavigator.title: The full-mouth navigator
fmxNavigator.body: While you view a single X-ray, the small grid at the top of the image is a map of the full-mouth series — the frame you're looking at is highlighted so you always know where you are. Hover it to expand to thumbnails (with coloured dots marking findings, and the panoramic in the centre), then click any frame to jump straight to that X-ray.

reportPrintout.title: Reports and printouts
reportPrintout.body: Report opens the AI's written summary for the study. Printout builds a shareable document: choose the Insurance Documentation or Patient Printout tab, how many images per page, portrait or landscape, and JPG or PDF; switch individual pathologies on or off, add tooth numbering or tooth parts, and tick which images to include. Download saves the JPG or opens the print dialog for a PDF.

photos.title: Patient photos
photos.body: Photos are ordinary camera pictures (intraoral or facial) — not X-rays, so no AI runs on them. Add one from New Study → Photo, or with Add photo on an empty Photos tab. Photos appear as a gallery; select one to view it full-size, move between them with the arrows, and delete the ones you don't need.

patientRecord.title: The patient record
patientRecord.body: A patient's page groups their work into X-rays, 3D and Photos tabs. The date button filters everything to one capture date (or All dates). For X-rays, the FMX switch toggles between the anatomical full-mouth grid — select any position to open the X-ray that fills it — and a simple list. Add study opens an upload already linked to this patient, Printout makes a study overview, and each tile can be opened or deleted.

cbctNavigate.title: Reading a CBCT — views and slices
cbctNavigate.body: Switch between MPR (three slice planes), 3D, Panoramic and Report with the tabs or the layout buttons on the left rail. In MPR the rail adds slice tools — crosshair to link the planes, pan, window/level, linear and angle measurements, an annotation pin and a slab (MIP) thickness — plus reset buttons. Scrub slices with each pane's slider, the mouse wheel, or the ↑/↓ keys over a pane (Ctrl/Cmd+scroll zooms), and maximise a pane to fill the view. Window and Level and Reduce noise tune the image; on an un-segmented scan, Run AI Segmentation builds the 3D model and report.

cbct3d.title: The CBCT 3D model
cbct3d.body: Drag to orbit, scroll to zoom and right-drag to pan. The orientation cube snaps the camera to a standard view. Click a tooth to select and highlight it (the rest fade back); double-click it to open its conditions. The Layers panel shows or hides each structure — individual teeth, the jaws and the canals.

cbctPanoramic.title: The CBCT panoramic view
cbctPanoramic.body: A panoramic reconstruction rebuilt from the volume. The tool strip offers a crosshair reference marker, pan, a millimetre ruler, a MIP slab thickness you can cycle, zoom-to-fit, and a switch to show or hide the overlays. Scroll to zoom and double-click to clear your markup.

cbctReport.title: The CBCT report
cbctReport.body: The report lists findings by tooth. The category chips (Periodontal, Restorative, Endodontic, Surgical) filter the list and chart; select a tooth on the chart to drill into its findings. For each tooth you can open its full Condition list, add a Comment, and Approve it. Print report opens a printable copy, and Approve all and sign records your sign-off.

readIos.title: Reading an IOS scan
readIos.body: The left rail snaps the model to standard views (anterior, posterior, right and left buccal, upper and lower occlusal), toggles a wireframe, measures distances on the surface (Clear measurements removes them), takes a screenshot, and resets the view. The layer buttons show All, Upper or Lower teeth, or hide individual teeth; selecting a tooth on the model or the chart highlights it in both. On a raw scan, Run AI Segmentation labels each tooth.

settings.title: Settings
settings.body: Choose your theme (Light, Dark or System) and language (English, French, German or Spanish) for this device. Set the tooth-numbering system (Universal or FDI), whether patient info shows by default, and the AI confidence threshold — lower is more sensitive (more findings, more false positives). The measurement-unit control is shown but not used: 2D X-rays carry no calibrated scale, and 3D measurements are always in millimetres.

account.title: Your profile
account.body: Update the name, mobile number and address kept on your account (used for receipts and recovery) and select Save changes. Your sign-in email is shown but can't be edited here.

billing.title: Subscription and billing
billing.body: AI inference is a subscription feature. With no active plan, the available plans are listed and Subscribe opens secure Stripe checkout. With an active plan you can see the renewal date and Cancel subscription (it stays active until the period ends). If you try to run AI without a plan, a dialog offers View plans or Not now.

home.title: Home dashboard
home.body: Your landing page. The quickest way to analyse an X-ray is front and centre; alongside it you can create a patient, search, review your most recent analyses, and see key totals.

demo.auth_signin_email: The email field for your account. Type your sign-in address here.
demo.auth_signin_password: Your account password. Selecting Sign in below authenticates.
demo.auth_signin_submit: Signs you in with the email and password above. On success you land on the studies dashboard.
demo.auth_signin_useCode: Switches the form to one-time-code sign-in: enter your email, receive a 6-digit code, then verify.
demo.auth_signin_usePassword: Switches back from email-code sign-in to the standard email + password form.
demo.auth_otp_sendCode: Emails a 6-digit one-time code to the address above. The form switches to the code-entry step.
demo.auth_otp_code: Type the 6-digit code from your email here. The countdown shows how long the code stays valid.
demo.auth_otp_verify: Verifies the entered code and signs you in. Successful verification takes you to the studies dashboard.
demo.auth_otp_requestNew: Requests a fresh 6-digit code if the previous one expired. Appears when the countdown reaches zero.
demo.auth_link_createAccount: Opens the sign-up form to create a brand-new account.
demo.auth_link_forgot: Opens the password-reset form. Enter your email there and a reset link is emailed to you.
demo.auth_signup_name: Your full name as it should appear on receipts and your account profile.
demo.auth_signup_email: Your work email — this becomes your sign-in address.
demo.auth_signup_confirm: Re-type your password. The form checks both fields match before letting you submit.
demo.auth_signup_acceptTerms: Confirms you accept the Terms of Service and Privacy Policy. Required before Create account is allowed.
demo.auth_signup_submit: Creates your account and takes you straight to the billing screen so you can choose a plan.
demo.auth_forgot_email: The email of the account you can't sign into. Send reset link emails a recovery URL there.
demo.auth_forgot_submit: Emails a password-reset link to the address above. A confirmation appears once it has been sent.
demo.auth_forgot_back: Returns to the sign-in form without sending a reset link.
demo.modals_consent_agree: Confirms you've read the terms (first sign-in or after an update). The dialog closes and the app continues.
demo.modals_consent_decline: Declines the terms and signs you out. You can return any time and accept later.
demo.workspace_sidebar_home: The BeCertain logo. Selecting it always returns you to the studies dashboard.
demo.workspace_sidebar_collapse: Collapses the sidebar to icons only, freeing horizontal space. Select again to expand.
demo.workspace_sidebar_help: Opens this manual. The active link is highlighted with a brand-coloured indicator.
demo.workspace_sidebar_theme: Toggles between light and dark themes for this device. The icon flips to indicate the current mode.
demo.workspace_sidebar_userMenu: Opens the user menu with Account, Billing, Settings and Log out.
demo.workspace_menu_account: Opens your profile (name, mobile, address). Your sign-in email is shown there but read-only.
demo.workspace_menu_billing: Opens the subscription page where you can manage your plan and renewal date.
demo.workspace_menu_settings: Opens this device's settings (theme, language, tooth-numbering, PHI default, AI confidence).
demo.workspace_menu_logout: Signs you out and returns you to the sign-in screen. Clears any cached patient data for the previous user.
demo.workspace_topbar_search: Filters the studies dashboard by patient name or date of birth as you type.
demo.workspace_topbar_history: Drops down a list of recently viewed studies. Select an entry to jump straight back to it.
demo.workspace_topbar_historyClear: Empties the recently-viewed list. The history is local to this browser.
demo.workspace_topbar_phi: Hides or shows patient-identifying information (names, DOB). Useful for screen-sharing; the setting persists across pages.
demo.studies_newStudy: Opens the New Study form for uploading a radiograph, scan or photo.
demo.studies_patientCard: Selecting a card opens that patient's record (X-rays, 3D, Photos).
demo.studies_retry: Re-fetches the studies list after a connection error.
demo.studies_clearSearch: Empties the search box and shows every patient again.
demo.studies_pager: When a list runs longer than one page, a pager appears at the bottom — Previous / Next buttons and a “Page X of Y · N results” status. The dashboard, a patient's image lists and the photo gallery all paginate this way, so even records with hundreds of studies stay fast.
demo.upload_modXray: Picks intraoral periapical or bitewing X-ray. The dropzone then accepts .jpg, .png or .webp.
demo.upload_modPanoramic: Picks a panoramic X-ray. The AI processes it through the 2D inference pipeline.
demo.upload_modCbct: Picks a CBCT (3D) volume. The dropzone accepts .nii.gz, .nrrd, .mha or .gipl.
demo.upload_modIos: Picks an intraoral scan mesh. The dropzone accepts .obj, .stl or .ply.
demo.upload_modPhoto: Picks a plain camera photo (intraoral or facial). No AI runs on photos and there is no paywall.
demo.upload_name: The patient name. As you type, matching existing patients appear below — selecting one files the study under that patient instead of creating a new record.
demo.upload_dob: Date of birth for a new patient. Capped at today (a future date is rejected).
demo.upload_phonePhoto: Tick this when the file is a phone snap of an X-ray on a monitor — the AI locates and crops the X-ray region before analysing it.
demo.upload_browse: Opens the OS file picker filtered to the right file types for the chosen modality.
demo.upload_dropzone: Drop a file here to select it for upload. The accepted extensions are listed underneath.
demo.upload_remove: Clears the selected file so you can pick a different one.
demo.upload_run: Starts the analysis: encodes the image, runs the AI, saves the study and opens it in the matching viewer. A progress bar shows the stage.
demo.upload_cancel: Discards the in-progress form and returns to the studies dashboard.
demo.quick_drag: Drag any file from your desktop into the Dx Viewer window. A 'Drop to analyze' overlay appears and analysis starts the moment you release.
demo.quick_paste: Copy an image or 3D file from the OS clipboard, then press Ctrl/Cmd+V anywhere outside a text field — Dx Viewer analyses it immediately.
demo.quick_screenCapture: Press Ctrl/Cmd+V with nothing to paste and the browser asks which screen, window or tab to capture. The captured frame is analysed as an X-ray.
demo.quick_assign_nameIt: Opens a tiny form to give the quick scan a real patient name and DOB. The temporary record is renamed in place.
demo.quick_assign_addExisting: Opens a patient-picker so you can file the quick scan under an existing patient instead of creating a new record.
demo.quick_assign_keep: Leaves the temporary patient as-is and dismisses the banner. You can rename it later from the patient record.
demo.quick_assign_nameInput: The name field for the rename form. Required before Save is enabled.
demo.quick_assign_dobInput: Optional date of birth for the rename form.
demo.quick_assign_save: Commits the entered name and DOB, clearing the temporary flag from the patient.
demo.quick_assign_search: Filters the existing-patient list by name as you type.
demo.quick_assign_assign: Moves every study from the temporary record onto the selected existing patient, then deletes the empty placeholder.
demo.quick_assign_cancel: Closes the rename or assign form and returns to the temporary banner.
demo.viewer2d_fmxToggle: Returns to the patient page so you can see every X-ray in the full-mouth grid.
demo.viewer2d_tabXrays: Shows the current X-ray and the toolbar. The count is the patient's total X-ray and panoramic studies.
demo.viewer2d_tabPhotos: Switches the workspace to the patient's photo gallery (no AI overlays).
demo.viewer2d_printout: Opens the Printout modal where you build an insurance or patient export with the images and pathologies you choose.
demo.viewer2d_report: Opens the AI's written diagnostic report for this X-ray.
demo.viewer2d_phi: Hides or shows the patient name and identifying info on this screen. The setting persists across the app.
demo.viewer2d_prev: Opens the previous X-ray in the patient's series.
demo.viewer2d_next: Opens the next X-ray in the patient's series.
demo.viewer2d_fullFmx: Returns to the patient's full-mouth grid view.
demo.viewer2d_arrowKeys: Pressing ← or → moves to the previous or next X-ray — the same as the on-screen arrows.
demo.viewer2d_zoomIn: Magnifies the image about its current centre. Mouse-wheel on the canvas does the same.
demo.viewer2d_zoomOut: Shrinks the image about its current centre.
demo.viewer2d_fit: Re-fits the image so the whole X-ray is visible at maximum size inside the viewport.
demo.viewer2d_actualSize: Sets the zoom to 100% — one image pixel maps to one screen pixel.
demo.viewer2d_magnifier: Activates a floating loupe: a small magnified circle follows your cursor over the X-ray.
demo.viewer2d_adjust: Opens a popover with brightness, contrast, sharpness and saturation sliders that adjust the image live.
demo.viewer2d_invert: Inverts the grayscale so dark and light areas swap — sometimes makes early caries easier to see.
demo.viewer2d_ruler: Activates measure mode. Drag from one anatomical point to another to draw a labelled line in pixels.
demo.viewer2d_toothNumbers: Overlays each detected tooth's number on the X-ray.
demo.viewer2d_anatomy: Overlays the AI's anatomy and tooth-part segmentation as coloured masks.
demo.viewer2d_resetAdjust: Clears every image adjustment (brightness, contrast, rotation, flip, inversion, rulers) and returns to the original view.
demo.viewer2d_fdiToggle: Switches the tooth-number labels between Universal (1–32) and FDI (11–48) on this screen.
demo.viewer2d_pan: Click and drag the X-ray (when not in measure or magnify mode) to slide it around the viewport.
demo.viewer2d_wheelZoom: Scroll the mouse wheel over the X-ray to zoom toward the cursor.
demo.viewer2d_adjust_brightness: Slides the brightness from dimmer to brighter; the canvas updates as you move the handle.
demo.viewer2d_adjust_contrast: Slides the contrast from flat to punchy; differences between tissue densities are amplified.
demo.viewer2d_adjust_sharpness: Applies an SVG convolution filter that sharpens edges — useful for fine cervical caries detail.
demo.viewer2d_adjust_saturation: Adjusts colour saturation. Mainly affects the AI overlays (the X-ray itself is grayscale).
demo.viewer2d_adjust_reset: Returns all four image-adjustment sliders to their defaults.
demo.viewer2d_findings_overlay: Turns the AI finding overlay (coloured masks and boxes) on or off as a whole.
demo.viewer2d_findings_allPathology: Toggles every pathology class (caries, bone loss, calculus, …) on or off at once.
demo.viewer2d_findings_classToggle: Each finding class (Caries, Bone Loss, Calculus, Periapical Radiolucency, …) has its own row — turn it off to hide just that class on the canvas.
demo.viewer2d_findings_toothParts: Overlays the AI's anatomical tooth-part masks (the same toggle as the Anatomy tool on the rail).
demo.viewer2d_report_close: Closes the diagnostic-report dialog and returns to the viewer.
demo.viewer2d_printout_tabInsurance: Generates an insurance-documentation header on the exported document.
demo.viewer2d_printout_tabPatient: Generates a patient-facing printout header on the exported document.
demo.viewer2d_printout_layout: Chooses how many X-rays fit on each page — one, two, four, or all of them.
demo.viewer2d_printout_orientation: Switches the page between portrait and landscape.
demo.viewer2d_printout_format: Picks the output format: JPG saves a single image, PDF opens the browser print dialog.
demo.viewer2d_printout_pathologies: Switches individual pathology classes on or off in the printed images — handy for hiding non-clinical detail from a patient view.
demo.viewer2d_printout_toothNumbering: Burns the tooth numbers into the printed images.
demo.viewer2d_printout_toothParts: Burns the anatomy and tooth-part masks into the printed images.
demo.viewer2d_printout_selectImages: Tap each thumbnail to include or exclude that X-ray from the printout. The current count appears in the label.
demo.viewer2d_printout_download: Builds the composite document and saves it as JPG, or opens the browser print dialog for PDF.
demo.fmx_grid_slot: Each anatomical slot in the full-mouth grid is selectable: it opens the X-ray that occupies that position. Filled slots show coloured corner dots marking the finding categories present.
demo.fmx_navigator: A compact map of the full-mouth series at the top of the X-ray viewer — the frame you're viewing is outlined.
demo.fmx_navigator_pick: Hover the navigator to expand it to thumbnails with finding-colour dots and the central panoramic; select any frame to jump straight to that X-ray.
demo.photos_add: Opens the New Study form pre-set to Photo modality, scoped to this patient.
demo.photos_view: Selecting a photo thumbnail opens a full-size lightbox view with the capture date.
demo.photos_delete: Removes the photo after a confirmation prompt.
demo.photos_navigate: Inside the lightbox, the arrow buttons (or ← / →) move to the previous or next photo.
demo.photos_close: Closes the lightbox and returns to the gallery.
demo.patient_fmxToggle: Switches the X-rays tab between the anatomical full-mouth grid and a simple thumbnail list.
demo.patient_dateFilter: Filters every list on the patient page to a single capture date, or shows All dates.
demo.patient_tabXrays: Shows the X-ray view (FMX grid or list).
demo.patient_tab3d: Shows the CBCT and IOS studies for this patient. Only appears when the patient has 3D scans.
demo.patient_addStudy: Opens the upload form already locked to this patient — any new study attaches to this record by id.
demo.patient_printout: Opens a printable overview of this patient's studies (respecting the date filter).
demo.patient_openStudy: Each tile opens the matching viewer — X-ray, CBCT or IOS.
demo.patient_deleteStudy: Removes the study after a confirmation prompt. The tile's trash icon appears on hover.
demo.cbct_viewMpr: Switches to the 2×2 grid of orthogonal slice planes (axial, sagittal, coronal) plus the 3D corner.
demo.cbct_view3d: Switches to the dedicated 3D segmentation view with the orientation cube.
demo.cbct_viewPanoramic: Switches to the panoramic reconstruction view.
demo.cbct_viewReport: Switches to the AI-written report with the tooth chart and per-tooth detail.
demo.cbct_runSegmentation: Runs the CBCT AI on a raw volume. Progress is shown for up to 5 minutes; afterwards the 3D model and report are available.
demo.cbct_tool_crosshair: Click or drag on any pane to move the linked crosshair to that point — the other two planes update to show that location.
demo.cbct_tool_pan: Drag inside a pane to slide the slice within its viewport without changing the slice index.
demo.cbct_tool_wl: Drag horizontally to widen or narrow the window, vertically to change the level. The image contrast updates live.
demo.cbct_tool_measure: Drag from one point to another to commit a millimetre-labelled line. Multiple measurements can coexist.
demo.cbct_tool_angle: Click three points (vertex, ray, ray) to commit an angle in degrees.
demo.cbct_tool_annotate: Click anywhere to drop an annotation pin and type a short note (Enter commits, Escape cancels).
demo.cbct_tool_slab: Cycles the MIP slab half-thickness (off → 3 → 6 → 10 voxels). Thicker slabs combine multiple slices for a smoother projection.
demo.cbct_tool_toggleCrosshair: Shows or hides the crosshair overlay across all three panes.
demo.cbct_tool_resetWL: Restores the default window and level.
demo.cbct_tool_resetAll: Clears every adjustment: window/level, zoom, crosshair, per-pane pan and zoom, all markups, and the 3D camera.
demo.cbct_mpr_sliceSlider: Each pane has its own slider — drag to scrub through the slices of that plane. The counter shows position / total.
demo.cbct_mpr_wheelScrub: Roll the mouse wheel over a pane to step ±1 slice. Hold Ctrl/Cmd to zoom instead.
demo.cbct_mpr_arrowScrub: Hover a pane, then press ↑ or ↓ to scrub that pane. The active pane is the one under your cursor.
demo.cbct_mpr_maximize: Each pane has a maximise button (top-right) that fills the whole MPR area with that pane. Select it again to restore the 2×2 grid.
demo.cbct_vol_orbit: Drag the 3D model to rotate it, scroll to zoom, right-drag to pan.
demo.cbct_vol_gizmo: Each face of the orientation cube (Top, Front, R, Bot, Back, L) snaps the camera to that anatomical view.
demo.cbct_vol_selectTooth: A single click on a tooth selects it — the other meshes fade back so you can see the selected one in context.
demo.cbct_vol_openTooth: A double click on a tooth opens the conditions modal listing every AI finding for that tooth with confidence percentages.
demo.cbct_layers_toggle: Each structure in the Layers panel (every tooth, the jaws, each canal) has its own checkbox — turn it off to hide that mesh.
demo.cbct_reduceNoise: Drops tiny disconnected mesh islands so the 3D model looks cleaner.
demo.cbct_window: The Window slider sets the contrast range. Move it to compress or expand which Hounsfield values map to visible greys.
demo.cbct_level: The Level slider shifts the centre of the contrast range. Together with Window it sets the 'tissue type' you're highlighting.
demo.cbct_pano_crosshair: Click on the panoramic to drop a cyan reference cross marking a point of interest.
demo.cbct_pano_pan: Drag to translate the panoramic image inside the viewport.
demo.cbct_pano_ruler: Drag from one point to another to commit a labelled millimetre measurement (voxel-spacing accurate).
demo.cbct_pano_slab: Cycles the MIP slab half-thickness (10 → 20 → 32 → 48 voxels) — the panoramic re-renders with the chosen slab.
demo.cbct_pano_zoomFit: Re-fits the panoramic to the canvas.
demo.cbct_pano_overlays: Hides or shows your markup (rulers, crosshair) on the panoramic.
demo.cbct_report_print: Opens a light-themed printable copy of the report and the browser print dialog.
demo.cbct_report_sign: Records your sign-off on the report with the current date. The button is replaced with a 'Signed' badge.
demo.cbct_report_categoryChips: Each chip (Periodontal, Restorative, Endodontic, Surgical) filters the findings list and tooth chart to that category. Select it again to clear the filter.
demo.cbct_report_toothChart: Each tooth in the chart is selectable. Picking one drills the right column into that tooth's findings, comment and approval state.
demo.cbct_report_selectTeeth: When at least one tooth is selected from the chart, this clears the selection so the chart shows the AI's top-N teeth again.
demo.cbct_card_condition: Opens the full Conditions list modal for the tooth: every detected condition with its confidence.
demo.cbct_card_comment: Opens an inline note field where you can type a clinical comment for the tooth. Save persists it; the comment also appears in the printed report.
demo.cbct_card_approve: Marks the tooth's findings as approved. The button changes to 'Approved'; selecting it again undoes the approval.
demo.cbct_modal_sort: Tabs at the top of the Conditions modal switch between grouping by category and a flat alphabetical list.
demo.cbct_modal_close: Closes the Conditions modal (the backdrop and the X close it too).
demo.ios_runSegmentation: Runs the AI on a raw IOS scan. Once it finishes, individual teeth become coloured and selectable.
demo.ios_view_anterior: Snaps the camera to the front of the model.
demo.ios_view_posterior: Snaps the camera to the back of the model.
demo.ios_view_rightBuccal: Snaps the camera to the right buccal side.
demo.ios_view_leftBuccal: Snaps the camera to the left buccal side.
demo.ios_view_occlusalUpper: Snaps the camera looking down onto the upper arch.
demo.ios_view_occlusalLower: Snaps the camera looking up at the lower arch.
demo.ios_wireframe: Toggles wireframe rendering — useful for inspecting mesh quality.
demo.ios_measure: Activates surface-measure mode. Two clicks on the mesh commit a millimetre-labelled line that follows the surface.
demo.ios_clearMeasure: Removes every committed surface measurement and exits measure mode. Only visible when at least one measurement exists.
demo.ios_screenshot: Saves the current 3D view as a PNG named after the patient and study.
demo.ios_resetView: Returns the camera to the default framing.
demo.ios_layers_all: Shows every tooth on the mesh.
demo.ios_layers_upper: Hides the lower arch (FDI 31–48), leaving only the upper teeth visible.
demo.ios_layers_lower: Hides the upper arch (FDI 11–28), leaving only the lower teeth visible.
demo.ios_layers_toothToggle: Each individual tooth has its own checkbox in the Layers panel — turn it off to hide just that tooth's mesh.
demo.ios_toothChart: Each cell in the chart selects that tooth on the 3D mesh. The selection is two-way — picking a tooth on the model highlights its chart cell.
demo.ios_selectTooth: Click any coloured tooth on the model to select it. Non-selected teeth dim back so the chosen one stands out.
demo.settings_theme: Three buttons — Light, Dark, System. The choice persists for this device. Imaging viewports stay dark in both themes for diagnostic consistency.
demo.settings_language: Switches the interface between English, French, Spanish and German. The choice persists for this device.
demo.settings_toothNumbering: Switches every tooth label between Universal (1–32) and FDI (11–48). Data stays FDI-keyed; only the displayed labels change.
demo.settings_confThreshold: A slider from 0.05 to 0.95 controlling the AI's display threshold. Lower means more sensitive (more findings, more false positives).
demo.settings_phiDefault: When ticked, patient identifying info shows by default on the dashboard and in the viewers.
demo.settings_measurementUnit: Shown for completeness only — the two buttons are disabled and labelled 'N/A for 2D'. 2D X-rays carry no calibrated scale; 3D measurements are always in millimetres.
demo.account_name: Your full name on file. Save changes commits the update.
demo.account_mobile: An optional contact number used for account recovery.
demo.account_address: An optional postal address used on receipts.
demo.account_save: Commits the form changes to your account. A green 'Profile updated' confirmation appears on success.
demo.billing_subscribe: Opens the secure Stripe checkout for the chosen plan in a new tab. After payment you're redirected back with the plan active.
demo.billing_cancel: Schedules the active plan to cancel at the end of the current billing period. The plan stays active until then.
demo.billing_cancelled: After you cancel, the plan does NOT stop immediately — it keeps working until the last paid day. The billing card then shows an “Ends at period end” badge and “Ends on <date>” in place of the renewal date, and the cancel button disappears. Full access continues until that date; only then does it revert to the no-subscription state.
demo.modals_paywall_viewPlans: Closes the dialog and opens the billing page so you can pick a plan.
demo.modals_paywall_notNow: Dismisses the dialog without leaving the current screen. You can run AI later once you subscribe.
demo.patient_edit: Opens a small form to correct this patient's name and date of birth, then saves the change.
demo.patient_delete: Permanently deletes the patient and every one of their studies, after a confirmation prompt.
demo.settings_enable3d: Turns on the CBCT and intraoral-scan (IOS) tools for your account. Off by default — with it off the app shows only X-rays and photos; turn it on to upload and view 3D studies. The setting follows your account across devices.
demo.viewer2d_editDetections: Adjust the AI's detections: the rectangle and freeform tools let you draw a new finding (then pick its type), and hovering an existing box lets you hide one you disagree with or drag its handles to resize it. Your edits are saved with the study and never overwrite the AI's original output.
demo.home_dropzone: Drop an X-ray here — or click to upload one — and AI analysis starts immediately. A patient is created automatically; you can name or merge it afterwards.
demo.home_createPatient: Opens the new-study form to add a patient and their first study.
demo.home_search: Search patients by name or date of birth; pick a match to jump to that patient, or press Enter to open the full list of results.
demo.home_recent: Your three most recent analyses — click one to reopen that patient.
demo.home_viewAll: Opens the full patient list, where you can search and browse every patient.
demo.home_metrics: At-a-glance totals for your account: total patients, analyses this week, and analyses overall.

localFirst.title: Your data stays on this device
localFirst.body: Dx Viewer does not store your patients or studies on our servers. They are saved only in this browser, on this device — losing the device or clearing your browser data will lose them. To keep a copy, use Settings → Labs → Local data to back them up to the server or export them to a file.

findings.detected: {count, plural, one {# finding detected} other {# findings detected}}
findings.measurementsTooltip: Bone-loss measurements (mm/%) require a calibrated scale and landmark detection that aren't available for 2D X-rays.

patient.deleteConfirm: Delete this patient and all {count} of their studies? This permanently removes the studies and can't be undone.
patient.deleteFailed: Couldn't delete the patient — please try again.

detect.saveFailed: Couldn't save your change. Please try again.

dashboard.dropHint: Drag an X-ray anywhere, or click to upload
